name: Chocolate Damnation
recipeInstructions:
Brownie Shell:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
2. Line a greased jelly roll pan with wax paper.
3. Melt butter and chocolate in a saucepan over low heat.
4. Remove from heat and add sugar and eggs. Combine flour, baking powder, and salt, then beat into the chocolate mixture. Add vanilla.
5. Pour batter into the pan and spread evenly.
6. Bake 12 to 15 minutes or until the cake springs back when lightly touched. It should be soft, not crisp.
7. Turn out on a rack and cool.
Filling:
1. Combine chocolate and coffee in a double boiler and melt over hot water.
2. Remove from heat and beat egg yolks until pale yellow. Stir some of the chocolate mixture into the yolks.
3. Return to the chocolate mixture and stir until smooth. Gradually add liqueur and cool.
4. Beat egg whites until foamy. Gradually add sugar and beat until stiff.
5. Whip cream until stiff and fold it into the cooled chocolate mixture, then fold in egg whites.
1. Invert the brownie shell onto a towel and peel off the paper.
2. Line the bottom and sides of a greased 9-inch square baking dish with the brownie shell, cutting strips for sides and using a 9-inch square for the bottom.
3. Turn filling into the cake-lined dish and cover with plastic wrap.
4. Chill for 3 to 4 hours or until firm.
Glaze:
1. Combine chocolate and coffee in a double boiler and melt over hot water.
1. Invert the chilled dessert onto a serving platter.
2. Coat with chocolate glaze.
3. Let the glaze set and decorate with chocolate curls.
4. Cut into very thin slices to serve.
